title: Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs 69 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ARC LANE COUNTY COMMUNITY CONNECTORS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Recruits and trains Direct Support Professionals (DSP) and then helps match them to children, youth, and adults who experience intellectual and developmental disabilities who may need support. The DSPs work one-on-one with children, youth, and adults on skill-building and inclusion services, both in the home and in the community. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 343-5256 107 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC LANE COUNTY THE ARC OF LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support and services to help people who experience intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Individuals with intellectual and/or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 343-5256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC UMATILLA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides connections to resources such as case management services, assistance with advocacy, disability awareness training, education, recreational programs, and vocational training. 215 W Orchard Avenue Hermiston, OR 978381738 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families Hours: Monday 9am-noon Email (541) 567-7615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAKER COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 2200 4th Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 523-8211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BARBARA ROSS BUILDING DEVELOPMENTAL DIVERSITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a range of support services to individuals experiencing intellectual and/or developmental disabilities. Services can include case management; individual and family support plans; access to brokerage services for transportation; in-home supports or residential placement; and employment and vocational services. 4185 SW Research Way Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals with an intellectual or developmental disability. Applicant must be an Oregon resident and a U.S. citizen. Support can include medication administration, financial paperwork and budgeting assistance, assistance with chores and cooking, and social supports. 96000 NE Cascades Parkway Suite 400 Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who are experiencing cerebral palsy or other intellectual and developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 777-4166 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services that may include networking and outreach, facilitation of Individual Support Plan (ISP) meetings, assisting in the referral process to community service agencies for supports, providing support to individuals and families in a variety of planning meetings, assisting in finding residential placement and employment services, as well as referrals to support services brokerages. The DD program staff may also review complaints of abuse and immediately take steps to assess further risk. In some cases, protective services may be offered to avoid further harm. 817 SE 13th Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must have the all of the following: -A disability that originated in the brain -The disability must be established prior the age of 22, or in the case of intellectual disability the condition must be determined before 18 years of age. -Must result in significant impairments in at least two of the following areas of daily function: Self-care, communication, cognition, mobility, self direction, capacity of independent living, and/or economic self sufficiency -The impairments must not be primarily related to: substance abuse, mental illness, an emotional disorder, ADHD, a learning disability, or sensory impairment. Impairments must be expected to last indefinitely.
